methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ursa Major Technologies is a Series E-stage Space & Aerospace company that has raised $400M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 4 private-sector peers at the same or adjacent stage (Astranis, ICEYE, Apex Space, and Stoke Space), with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $3.5B, alongside a broader public-market reference set of 5 Space & Aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 53.2x price-to-sales.

Two valuation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier to the $400M total raised proxy, implying a $560M post-money value. The peer-median method anchors to the $1.9B median valuation across the four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comps, with observed peer values spanning $1.0B to $3.5B. The public-comp anchoring approach cannot be applied due to the absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for private companies.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $476M (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.2B. The wide range reflects material uncertainty inherent in early-stage frontier-tech valuation: stage-progression mechanics suggest the tighter end, while peer medians reflect higher realized values in the sector. The sensitivity between methods underscores the criticality of forward revenue trajectory and capital efficiency in narrowing this band.
